nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Teaching for Results
nodes.description: Analysis of planning, curriculum design, teaching, evaluation and classroom management from the perspective of research and practice. Individual tasks culminate in production of a licensure portfolio. Credit not awarded for both EDSC 4300 and EDSC 5230. Prerequisites: EDSC 1110, EDSC 2070, EDSC 2090, EDSC 2150, EDSC 2160. Co-requisite: EDSC 4991.
nodes.other_requirements: Secondary Ed students only - SE, SEE, SEL, SEM, SES, SESS; Senior standing.

nodes.title: Disciplinary Literacy Sec Schl
nodes.description: Theory and methods of disciplinary literacy for teaching in secondary schools. Focus on literacy skills and critical thinking across disciplines. Cultural contexts explored. Pre/co-requisites: EDSC 5207, EDSC 5209, EDSC 5216.
nodes.other_requirements: For secondary MAT and AMP students in good standing

nodes.title: Practicum in Teaching
nodes.description: Immerses students in the school environment. Coursework, readings and experiences are intentionally aligned with those of EDSC 216. Allows students to engage in fieldwork in a variety of instructional settings that include students with special needs and ELLs. A seminar component brings together fieldwork and professional practice experiences. Prerequisite: EDSC 2070. Co-requisite: EDSC 2016.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisite: EDSC 2016. Secondary Ed students only - SE, SEE, SEL, SEM, SES, SESS; Minimum junior standing; Lab fee: $40.00 Fulfillment of Praxis Core requirement; Field placement TBD w/ instructor typically T/R 4-hr blocks between 7:30-12:00 Course will also meet on campus T/R 9:00-11:30 as indicated by the instructor for the first two weeks of the semester. Afterwards, it will meet Thursdays 2:50-4:05

nodes.title: Tchg Math in Sec Schls
nodes.description: Designed for students interested in becoming mathematics educators. Focuses on mathematics teaching and learning at the middle and secondary level. Students will explore ways of probing student understanding, identify and evaluate best practices for instruction, and will examine issues of diversity and equity in the classroom. Credit not awarded for both EDSC 3570 and EDSC 5257.
nodes.other_requirements: Secondary MAT and Undergrad students; Minimum Jr standing;

nodes.title: Lang Policy Issues,Race&Sch
nodes.description: Explores the theories, practices, and policies related to race and language, and how they impact the working, teaching, and learning of English learners in multilingual and multicultural settings.

nodes.title: SL:Development: Theory & App
nodes.description: Examines a variety of adolescent developmental and theories about how students learn. On-going participation in Service-Learning experiences allows for the application of course content in the context of instructional settings, with an emphasis on supporting students identified as dealing with barriers to learning. Prerequisite: EDTE 1010, EDSP 1050, ECLD 1560, EDFS 1020, EDSC 1110.
nodes.other_requirements: Secondary Ed students only - SE, SEE, SEL, SEM, SES, SESS; Minimum Sophomore standing only; service learning req

nodes.title: Internship: Student Teaching
nodes.description: Student teaching or some other on-site supervised experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team, for which academic credit is awarded.
nodes.other_requirements: Secondary Ed students only. Senior standing; Co-requisite:EDSC 4300.

nodes.title: Intro to Teaching Math
nodes.description: Provides an introduction to the field of mathematics education. Explores the knowledge and skills required to teach middle and secondary mathematics, investigate how people learn mathematics, and study current issues and research in mathematics education.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission required to register.

nodes.title: Teach English:Secondary School
nodes.description: Approaches to teaching composition, literature, and the English language in secondary school. Prerequisite: Acceptance into licensure program.
nodes.other_requirements: Acceptance into licensure program. Secondary MAT and Undergrad students; Minimum Jr standing.

nodes.title: Teaching for Results
nodes.description: Analysis of planning, curriculum design, teaching, evaluation and classroom management from the perspective of research and practice. Individual tasks culminate in production of a licensure portfolio. Credit not awarded for both EDSC 5230 and EDSC 4300. Co-requisite: EDSC 4226.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing; Internships meet off campus; individual site placements

nodes.title: SL:Disciplinary Lit Sec Schl
nodes.description: Theory and methods of reading/writing explored in the context of literacy. Focus on reading, writing, speaking and critical thinking across disciplines. Cultural contexts explored. Pre/Co-requisites: EDSC 2070, EDSC 2090, EDSC 2016.
nodes.other_requirements: Secondary Ed students only - SE, SEE, SEL, SEM, SES, SESS; Minimum Junior standing only; Service learning req.; has a GC2 designation.

nodes.title: Internship: Student Teaching
nodes.description: Student teaching or some other on-site supervised experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team, for which academic credit is awarded.
nodes.other_requirements:
For secondary MAT students in good standing; Student teaching or some other on-site supervised experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team, for which academic credit is awarded. Internships meet off campus; individual site placements. Internship placement for Student Teaching. Attend all internship requirements over 13 weeks.
Submit lesson plans for observations
Complete Demograhics and Accomodations Matrices for placement
Communicate regularly with UVM supervisor and Mentor teacher
Plan and Facilitate 3 Triad meetings

nodes.title: Tchng Science in Sec Schls
nodes.description: Students explore best practices of teaching, learning, and assessing science in the middle and high school classroom. Leverages a professional learning community model to engage critical dialogue, reflection, and feedback about teaching scientific inquiry & the 3-dimensional learning practices of the NGSS. Various pedagogical approaches like place-based and project-based learning are also examined. Credit not awarded for both EDSC 3270 and EDSC 5227. Prerequisite: EDSC 1110, EDSC 2070, EDSC 2090, EDSC 2150, EDSC 2160.
nodes.other_requirements: Secondary MAT and Undergrad students; Minimum Jr standing; Prereq for MATs:EDSC 5207,5209,5215,5216; Synchronous online/In-Person components, specific dates TBA; Open to Degree and PACE Students

nodes.title: Tchg Soc Studies in Sec School
nodes.description: Students are introduced to the philosophy, aims, and methods of social studies teaching in secondary schools. Emphasis is placed on engaging all learners through active and equitable learning opportunities and developing critical perspectives in teaching. Fosters content knowledge, pedagogical knowledge, and pedagogical content knowledge across social studies disciplines. Credit not awarded for both EDSC 3250 and EDSC 5225. Prerequisites: EDSC 1110, EDSC 2070, EDSC 2090, EDSC 2150, EDSC 2160.
nodes.other_requirements: Secondary MAT and Undergrad students; Minimum Jr standing; Prereq for MATs:EDSC 5207,5209,5215,5216; Synchronous online and In-Person components; Specific dates TBA; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: CL:Tchng to Make a Difference
nodes.description: Serves as an introduction to the field of education and how teaching can foster a more just and humane world.

nodes.title: Soc, Hst & Phil Found of Educ
nodes.description: Critical examination of central educational/social issues and values with special emphasis on the struggle for justice and equality. Themes include schooling and social class, race, and gender; the purposes of education; and the responsibilities of teachers. Prerequisite: Completion of a 2000 level course.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: School and Society
nodes.description: As a foundational course in the College of Education and Social Services, this course investigates the interplay between schooling and society. We examine how children and youth are situated within the context of systems, including their families, communities, and the larger society, that influence their development and learning. We will explore the sociological purposes of schools, their historical development in the US, and the resulting inequitable impact of school organization and policy.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Iss Aff Persons W/Disabil
nodes.description: Students study the effects of discrimination, advocacy, litigation and sociological perspectives on disabilities. History, current legislation, and family issues for children and adults are emphasized.
nodes.other_requirements: Only open to CESS first-time first-years or by instructor permission; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Practicum in Teaching
nodes.description: Field-experience in secondary setting. Focus on school culture and student needs while documenting effectiveness in one-on-one teaching. Professional attributes/dispositions are critically assessed. Pre/co-requisites: EDFS 3030, EDSC 5207.
nodes.other_requirements: Field placement TBD with instructor; On campus component: Class to meet on Thursdays from 4:35 to 7:35; Lab fee: $40.00

nodes.title: Curriculum, Inst, & Assessment
nodes.description: Covers key concepts within education such as Understanding by Design ('backwards planning'), formative and summative assessment, differentiated instruction, and equity in the secondary content-area curriculum. Course readings, discussions, projects, and activities will illustrate how these concepts fit together to support student learning in secondary schools. Prerequisites: EDSC 1110; EDSC 2070. Co-requisite: EDSC 2090.
nodes.other_requirements: Secondary Ed students only - SE, SEE, SEL, SEM, SES, SESS; Minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: Curr,Instr&Assmt Sec Schl Tchr
nodes.description: Development of methods related to secondary school teaching. Study and application of constructivist learning theory, differentiation, authentic assessment in planning. Focus on cross-disciplinary collaboration. Co-requisite: EDSC 5215.
nodes.other_requirements: Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for EDSC 5216 ZRA; Co-located with EDSC 5216 ZRA and EDML 5600; Total combined enrollment: 25.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission only.

nodes.title: IS:Tchg ForeignLang inSecSchls
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
